text.title: Daily Standup AI Pre-Brief Template Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every Daily Standup AI Pre-Brief Template application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Pre-brief ≤400 words. Drafts exceeding cap = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Long pre-briefs are skipped; short ones change the meeting.

rule.statement: Pre-brief sends 30 minutes before standup. Send-time slip >5 min = flag.
Every section cites a specific source (ticket id, PR number, Slack thread). Untyped statements = REJECT.
Anomalies (PR failures, CI red, missed standup yesterday) are flagged in a dedicated section.
rule.rationale: Anomalies in the noise mean nobody acts on them.

rule.statement: Each block names an owner (engineer, PM, blocker-resolver). No anonymous blocks.
rule.rationale: Anonymous blocks are read but not acted on.

rule.statement: Pre-brief carries ISO date + sprint id. Pre-briefs append-only in `standups/` dir.
rule.rationale: Trend over weeks shows team rhythm; mutating breaks it.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
